prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of aniline
Method provenance: DFT at tier “standard” – B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: fixed at the provided aniline.xyz coordinates (no re-optimization). Single-point calculations were performed for the neutral (N), cation (N–1, doublet) and anion (N+1, doublet) states; Fukui functions were derived from Mulliken charges following the standard finite-difference scheme (f⁺ = q(N) – q(N–1), f⁻ = q(N+1) – q(N)).

Convergence & warnings: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true.
The engine issued three warnings (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

Key results (atomic indices are 1-based):
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C) f⁺ = 2.2975867
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (N) f⁻ = 0.20452359

Selected Fukui indices (f⁺ / f⁻ / dual descriptor f²):
1 N  1.6696628  0.20452359  1.4651392
3 C  2.2975867  0.066571393  2.2310153
… (full arrays in result JSON)

Live run artifacts: the calculation was executed with the command captured in the result’s cli_invocation string (see JSON) and streamed live to the automatically generated .out log in the working directory; this log can be inspected for full SCF details. Output files include the JSON (`aniline_fukui2.json`) and a PNG bar-chart of atomic Fukui values (`agent_call_02_plot.png`).

Overall, the computed Fukui functions identify the para carbon (atom 3) as the most electrophilic center and the amino nitrogen (atom 1) as the most nucleophilic site under gas-phase conditions at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level.
